description: Source IP observed generating repeated failed login attempts against the same Windows user account.
recommended_action: Verify source device ownership, review authentication activity, and block if unauthorized.
notes: Simulated lab indicator for SOC portfolio project.

description: User account targeted by multiple failed login attempts followed by one successful login.
recommended_action: Confirm activity with user, force password reset, and review recent sessions.
notes: Successful login after repeated failures increased the risk level.

description: Windows Security Event ID showing failed authentication attempts.
recommended_action: Correlate failed login count with source IP, username, and time window.
notes: 12 failed logins were observed within 5 minutes.

description: Windows Security Event ID showing a successful login after repeated failed attempts.
recommended_action: Review user activity after login and check for suspicious process execution.
notes: This event makes the case more serious than failed attempts alone.

description: Windows endpoint where suspicious authentication activity was observed.
recommended_action: Review local security logs and monitor for additional failed login attempts.
notes: Lab endpoint used for simulated SOC investigation.

description: Network logon type observed during failed and successful authentication events.
recommended_action: Check whether network logon from this source IP is expected.
notes: Logon Type 3 is commonly linked with network-based authentication.

indicator_value: 12 failed logins followed by 1 successful login
description: Authentication pattern consistent with possible brute force activity.
recommended_action: Escalate to SOC Level 2 and perform account compromise checks.
notes: Main suspicious pattern used in this case study.
